C-AMP AI Business Intelligence
# C-AMP AI Business Intelligence Expert — Comprehensive Project Summary
## Overview
This is an AI-powered business intelligence system built for the Western
Cape Government's Department of Infrastructure (DOI). It enables users 
to ask natural language questions about government facility data (from 
the Custodian Asset Management Plan — C-AMP) and receive SQL-generated 
answers as interactive charts, data tables, and AI-written business 
interpretations.
The system was authored by Heinrich Muller and is marked as proprietary 
intellectual property.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
## Architecture
A three-tier architecture with a React frontend, an Express.js API 
gateway, and a Python FastAPI + LangGraph backend:
React Frontend (dist only) → Express.js Gateway (:8000) → Python FastAPI 
Backend (:5000)
                                                                    │
                                                              SQLite Database
                                                         (new_camp_database.db ~19MB)
Deployed to Azure Container Apps in the South Africa North region.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
## 1. Frontend API Gateway (/api)
A lightweight Express.js proxy layer that sits between the frontend and 
the Python backend. It contains zero business logic — purely routing, 
auth, validation, and rate limiting.
### Tech Stack
- Express.js 4.x, Node 20- Helmet (security headers), CORS, Morgan (logging)
- express-openapi-validator — validates all requests against 
openapi.yaml
- swagger-ui-express — serves interactive API docs at /api-docs
- jsonwebtoken + jwks-rsa — Azure Entra ID (formerly Azure AD) JWT 
authentication
- express-rate-limit — 60 req/min global, 10 req/min on query endpoints
- Axios for backend communication (120s timeout)
- Jest + Supertest for testing
### Authentication
Uses Azure Entra ID (Microsoft identity platform). The auth.js 
middleware:
- Fetches JWKS signing keys from login.microsoftonline.com
- Verifies JWT tokens with RS256 algorithm
- Validates audience (api://{CLIENT_ID}), issuer, and tenant ID
- Requires access_as_user scope
- All /api/chat/* , /api/models, and /api/export/* routes are protected
### API Endpoints (from openapi.yaml)
| Method | Path | Auth | Description |
|--------|------|------|-------------|
| GET | / | No | Service info |
| GET | /api/health | No | Health check (pings backend) |
| POST | /api/chat/query | Yes | Submit natural language query |
| POST | /api/chat/new | Yes | Start new chat session (stub) |
| POST | /api/chat/clear | Yes | Clear chat session (stub) |
| GET | /api/models | Yes | List available LLM models |
| POST | /api/export/pdf | Yes | Export chat as PDF |
### Error Handling
- Backend connection refused → 502
- Backend timeout → 504
- All errors normalized to { status: 'error', message, errors? }
### File Structure
api/
├── server.js                    # Entry point
├── openapi.yaml                 # OpenAPI 3.0 contract (source of truth)
├── src/
│   ├── app.js                   # Express app assembly
│   ├── gateway/backendClient.js # Axios instance to backend
│   ├── middleware/
│   │   ├── auth.js              # Azure Entra ID JWT verification
│   │   ├── rateLimit.js         # Global + query rate limiters
│   │   └── errorHandler.js      # Centralized error handler│   ├── routes/                  # Thin route definitions
│   └── controllers/             # Request handlers (proxy to backend)
├── tests/app.test.js            # Jest integration tests
├── docs/
│   ├── architecture.md          # Architecture decisions
│   └── create_api_gateway_instructions.md  # AI scaffolding template
└── Dockerfile                   # Node 20 Alpine
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
## 2. Python Backend (/backend)
The core intelligence layer. A FastAPI server that orchestrates a multi-
agent LangGraph workflow to convert natural language into SQL, execute 
queries, generate Plotly visualizations, and produce AI-driven business 
interpretations.
### Tech Stack
- FastAPI + Uvicorn
- LangGraph (state machine workflow orchestration)
- LangChain + LangChain-OpenAI (LLM integration)
- SQLAlchemy + SQLite (database)
- Pandas + NumPy (data processing)
- Plotly + Kaleido (visualization + static image export)
- ReportLab + PyPDF2 (PDF generation)
- Azure Identity (Managed Identity auth for Azure OpenAI)
- Python 3.10/3.11
### LLM Support
Configurable via configv8.yaml. Supports:
- Azure OpenAI (with API key or Managed Identity)
- OpenAI direct: GPT-4.1, GPT-4.1-mini, GPT-4o, GPT-4o-mini
- DeepSeek: V3.1 Thinking Mode (reasoner) and Non-thinking Mode (chat)
Default model: gpt-4.1-mini, temperature: 0.1
### Multi-Agent LangGraph Workflow
The core pipeline is a compiled StateGraph with these nodes:
START → preprocess_routing → generate_sql → convert_dataframe →
  ┌─ [chart] → instruct_chart_generator → generate_chart ─┐
  │                                                         │
  └─ [table] ─────────────────────────────────────
─────────┘
                          ↓
              generate_interpretation → state_printer → END
#### Node Details:
1. preprocess_routing — Classifies user intent (chart vs table). Also 
handles meta-queries directly without SQL:
   - "List abbreviations" → returns abbreviation dictionary
   - "List concepts/definitions" → returns concept dictionary
   - "What does CGI mean?" → returns specific term lookup
   - "List chart types" → returns supported chart types
   - "List fields in [table]" → returns schema info
   - Otherwise, invokes the LLM routing preprocessor (JSON output)
2. generate_sql — Schema-aware SQL generation with an extremely detailed 
prompt that includes:
   - Full database schema with exact column names and case-sensitivity 
rules
   - Automatic NULL filtering for chart queries (mandatory)
   - Financial year defaulting (auto-adds latest year if not specified)
   - Conversation context for follow-up questions
   - Domain abbreviations and concepts
   - Chart-specific SQL patterns (maps need lat/lon, histograms need raw
values, Sankey needs UNION ALL, etc.)
   - PRINTF formatting rules (never for charts, allowed for tables)
   - SQL injection prevention rules
   - Specific error handling for API key, quota, and rate limit errors
3. convert_dataframe — Executes the SQL via DatabaseManager (thread-safe,
fresh connection per query, retry logic, validation). Returns data as 
dict records.
4. decide_chart_or_table — Conditional edge. Falls back to checking the 
original question for chart keywords as a safety net.
5. instruct_chart_generator — LLM generates natural language chart 
instructions from the data and question.
6. generate_chart — ChartTemplateManager detects chart type, generates 
Python/Plotly code, executes it via exec(), and serializes the figure to
JSON.
7. generate_interpretation — Two modes:
   - Standard: 2 paragraphs + 3-7 bullet points   - Detailed: 5 sections (Key Findings, Comparisons, Business 
Implications, Risk Indicators, Actionable Recommendations) with 
benchmarks
   - Uses strategic sampling: top 10, bottom 10, and 50 random middle 
rows
   - Includes full dataset statistics and categorical summaries
8. state_printer — Debug logging of final state.
### Chart Types (14 supported)
Bar, Line, Indexed Line, Scatter, Bubble, Pie, Doughnut, Histogram, 
Faceted Histogram, Box Plot, Heatmap, Correlation Heatmap, Map (
OpenStreetMap with lat/lon), Sankey Diagram
### Database
- SQLite file: database/new_camp_database.db (~19MB)
- Main tables: CAMP_Master_Facility_Summary, CAMP_Performance, 
CAMP_Lifecycle, AFS, AFS_Inactive_Assets, AFS_Transactions
- Key fields: Asset Name, Facility Type, CGI ratings (International and 
GIAMA scales), Remaining Useful Life, Maintenance Backlog, CRC, Lat/Lon 
coordinates, Financial Year, Management Region
### Security
- SQL injection protection via SQLValidator (blocked patterns: DROP, 
DELETE, UPDATE, INSERT, ALTER, UNION ALL SELECT)
- Allowed tables whitelist
- Query timeout: 30s, max rows: 10,000
- Blocked SQL patterns (regex-based)
- API keys stored in .env (gitignored)
### Domain Context (configv8.yaml)
The config file is the knowledge backbone — 19KB of domain-specific 
context:
- 20 abbreviations (HI, EI, GI, FCA, RUL, CRC, GIAMA, CGI, C-AMP, U-AMP,
etc.)
- 30+ concept definitions covering: Facility Condition Assessment, 
Remaining Useful Life, Maintenance Backlog, GIAMA Act, CGI scales (
International vs GIAMA — critically different: International 1=best/5=
worst, GIAMA 1=worst/5=best), FPI actions, CRC calculations, 
deterioration formulas, and more
### PDF Export (pdf_exportv8.py)
- Uses ReportLab for PDF generation
- Converts Plotly figures to PNG via Kaleido
- Structures: Header → User Question → SQL Query → Chart/Table → 
Interpretation → Footer
- Supports multi-turn conversation export### API Endpoints
| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | / | Health check |
| GET | /models | List available LLM models |
| POST | /query | Process natural language query |
| POST | /export-pdf | Export chat as PDF |
### Database Schema Test (/db_schema_test)
A standalone Docker container that connects to a PostgreSQL database via
Azure Managed Identity, introspects the schema, and validates that all 
expected tables exist. This suggests the production deployment uses 
PostgreSQL (not SQLite).

## Key Observations
1. The codebase is at version 8.0 — it's been through significant 
iteration
2. The agent files are large (~2300 and ~3300 lines respectively) with 
extensive prompt engineering3. The SQL generation prompt is extremely detailed (~500 lines) with 
many examples and edge cases, reflecting real-world debugging of LLM SQL
generation issues
4. The system has dual CGI scales (International vs GIAMA) which are 
inverted — this is a critical domain nuance baked into every prompt
5. The chat/new and chat/clear endpoints are stubs (TODO) — session 
management isn't fully wired up
6. The frontend source code is missing from this repo — only the built 
dist is present
7. There's a commented-out older version of generate_sql still in the 
codebase
8. The requests.http file contains a full JWT token for testing (should 
be rotated)
FAR Chatbot:
FAR Chatbot
## DOI FAR Chatbot — Full Project Summary
This is a multi-service chatbot application built for the Western Cape 
Department of Infrastructure (DOI). Its purpose is to provide an AI-
powered conversational interface over the department's Facility Asset 
Register (FAR) — a dataset containing information about government-owned
schools, clinics, land parcels, and other infrastructure assets across 
the Western Cape, South Africa.
Users can ask natural language questions like "List all schools in the 
Cape Winelands District" or "What is the municipal value of Belhar 
Primary School?" and the chatbot translates those into SQL queries, 
retrieves the data, and responds conversationally.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
### 1. API (apps/api) — The Public Gateway
Tech stack: TypeScript, Express 5, Bun runtime
This is the only publicly-exposed service. It acts as a REST gateway 
between the web frontend and the chatbot backend. Key responsibilities:
- Authentication via Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD). The auth.ts 
middleware validates JWT tokens from the Authorization header against 
Microsoft's JWKS endpoint, verifies the tenant ID, and checks for the 
access_as_user scope. Every request must be authenticated — the user's 
oid (object ID) from the token becomes their userId.
- Chat session management via direct PostgreSQL queries (using the 
shared @doi-chatbot/shared package):
  - POST /api/chat — Creates a new chat session for the authenticated 
user
  - GET /api/chat — Lists all chats for the user, ordered by most 
recently updated
  - GET /api/chat/:chat_id — Retrieves all messages for a specific chat 
(with ownership verification)
  - DELETE /api/chat/:chat_id — Deletes a chat and its messages (cascade
)
  - PATCH /api/chat/:chat_id/name — Renames a chat
- Message handling (POST /api/chat/message):
  1. Validates the request body (chat_id, message_id, text, timestamp) using custom middleware validators and transformers (trims whitespace, 
checks types, checks for empty strings)
  2. Verifies the user owns the chat
  3. Saves the user's message to the messages table with a sequence 
number
  4. Forwards the message to the chatbot backend via 
langgraph.sendMessage() — an internal HTTP POST to 
LANGGRAPH_API_URL/query/
  5. Saves the bot's response back to the database
  6. Returns the response to the client
  7. Uses an activeChatRequests Set to prevent concurrent messages on 
the same chat (returns 429 if a message is already being processed)
- Swagger/OpenAPI docs served at /api-docs
- Debug logging middleware in DEV mode
- CORS enabled
The lib/ folder contains well-organized utilities:
- validators/body.ts — Express middleware for validating nested body 
parameters exist and are the correct type
- transformers/body.ts — Express middleware for recursively trimming 
string values in request bodies
- error_responses.ts — Standardized 422 error response helpers
- helpers.ts — UUID generation and timestamp utilities
- api_clients/langgraph.ts — HTTP client for the chatbot backend, sends 
message + chat_id + optional model/memory settings
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
### 2. Chatbot Backend (apps/chatbot-backend) — The AI Brain
Tech stack: TypeScript, LangChain/LangGraph, Azure OpenAI, Express, Bun 
runtime
This service is internal-only (not publicly accessible in production). 
It runs on port 4000 and exposes two endpoints:
- POST /query/ — Accepts a message, chat_id, optional model name, and 
short-term memory flag
- GET /status/ — Health check
There are two architectural approaches in the codebase (the active one 
uses the LangGraph prebuilt ReAct agent):
Active architecture (bot/agent.ts):
- Uses createReactAgent from @langchain/langgraph/prebuilt- Creates an Azure OpenAI LLM (AzureChatOpenAI) with configurable 
deployment names (supports models like gpt-4o-mini, gpt-4o, gpt-4.1, gpt
-4.1-mini, gpt-4.1-nano)
- Temperature set to 0 for deterministic responses
- Uses MemorySaver as a checkpointer for conversation memory (keyed by 
chat_id as thread_id)
- The agent is given a detailed system prompt that:
  - Identifies itself as an assistant for the Western Cape Department of
Infrastructure
  - Contains the full database schema description (3 tables: 
asset_envelope, facility, land_parcel)
  - Provides SQL query guidelines (always use ILIKE, always LIMIT 20, 
pagination support)
  - Handles greetings, capability questions, and off-topic redirects
Tools available to the agent:
1. execute_sql_query (active) — A DynamicTool that:
   - Only allows SELECT statements
   - Blocks dangerous operations (DROP, DELETE, INSERT, UPDATE, ALTER, 
CREATE, TRUNCATE)
   - Auto-appends LIMIT 20 if missing (unless it's a COUNT/aggregation 
query)
   - Connects to PostgreSQL via the shared connection pool
   - Returns JSON with row count and data
2. retrieve_from_vector_db (currently disabled) — A DynamicTool for 
semantic search using pgvector:
   - Uses AzureOpenAIEmbeddings for embedding queries
   - Searches the langchain_pg_embedding table using cosine distance
   - Returns formatted results with metadata/citations
   - Configurable k parameter for number of results
Legacy architecture (bot/chatbot.ts + bot/graph.ts):
- A custom StateGraph implementation with explicit agent/tools nodes and
conditional routing
- More detailed ReAct prompt with extensive SQL examples, PostgreSQL-
specific syntax guidance, and 20+ join query examples
- Includes conversation window management (short-term memory = 12 
messages, default = 2)
- Max iterations safety (default 10, configurable to 100)
Schema definition (bot/schema.ts):
- Defines the 3 database tables and their columns as a TypeScript object
- asset_envelope — Asset envelope grouping (envelope_name, address, 
coordinates, department, municipality info)
- facility — Facilities like schools and clinics (facility_name, address, coordinates, linked to asset_envelope)
- land_parcel — Land parcels with financial data (municipal values, 
opening/closing balances, title deeds, registration dates, vesting 
status)

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
### 3. Web Frontend (apps/web) — The User Interface
Tech stack: React 19, Vite, Tailwind CSS 4, React Router
A single-page application with one route (/) rendering the Chatbot page.
Key components:
Chatbot.jsx — Main page layout:
- Full-screen with a custom SVG background
- Renders ChatHeader, message list, ChatInput, and Sidebar
- Supports PDF and Word export of chat conversations (using jsPDF for 
PDF, HTML blob for .doc)
useChat.js — Core chat hook managing all state:
- Manages messages, input, chat selection, loading state, model 
selection, and memory toggle
- On mount: fetches all existing chats, creates a new one if none exist
- New chats get a welcome message with 22 sample questions covering 
various query types
- handleSendMessage: sends user message, shows "..." typing indicator, 
calls API, then displays response with typing animation
- Typing animation: word-by-word display with adaptive speed (faster for
longer messages), tables are rendered instantly, sample questions shown
only after typing completes
- Auto-renames chat to the first user message (truncated to 50 chars)
- Supports loading, deleting, and renaming existing chats
- Rate limiting: shows error if a message is already being processed (
429)
- Auto-scrolls to bottom when user is already at the bottom
ChatMessage.jsx — Message rendering:
- UserMessage — Simple right-aligned bubble with user icon
- BotMessage — Left-aligned with bot icon, renders markdown via 
react-markdown with remark-gfm (supports tables, bold, lists, etc.)
  - Strips "Final Answer:" prefix from responses
  - Collapsible "References" section
  - Collapsible "Sample Questions" section with clickable questions that
populate the input fieldChatInput.jsx — Message input:
- Auto-resizing textarea (36px to 160px)
- Enter to send, Shift+Enter for newline
- Disabled during loading with spinner animation
- Send button activates only when input is non-empty
ChatSidebar.jsx — Settings and chat management panel:
- Collapsible sidebar with toggle button
- Chat list with search, inline rename (pencil icon), and delete (with 
confirmation)
- Relative timestamps ("Just now", "5m ago", "2h ago", "3d ago")
- Active chat highlighting
- Settings section:
  - Model selector dropdown (gpt-4.1-nano, gpt-4o-mini, gpt-4.1-mini, 
gpt-4o, gpt-4.1)
  - Short-term memory toggle
  - Clear chat history button
- Export buttons: PDF and Word
ChatHeader.jsx — Top navigation bar:
- eMerge logo/branding ("FAR Chatbot" title)
- New chat button (+ icon)
- User menu (currently commented out) — would show account info from 
/.auth/me and logout via /.auth/logout
Authentication — MSAL (Microsoft Authentication Library) integration is 
present but currently commented out in App.jsx and main.jsx. The 
msalConfig.js is configured with a specific client ID and tenant 
authority. When enabled, it would enforce SSO/popup login before showing
the app.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
### 4. Shared Package (apps/shared)
Published as @doi-chatbot/shared (workspace dependency). Contains:
- db.ts — PostgreSQL connection management:
  - Connection pool (max 10 connections, 30s idle timeout, 5s connection
timeout)
  - Supports both local (no SSL) and Azure PostgreSQL (SSL required) 
based on hostname detection
  - Azure Managed Identity token acquisition for passwordless auth in 
production
  - Exports query(), getConnectionPool(), getConnectionString(), getConnectionConfig()
- types.ts — Shared TypeScript interfaces (ChatMessage, ChatSession)
- utils.ts — formatDate() and generateId() utilities
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
### 5. Vector Database (vector-database/)
- PostgreSQL 17 with pgvector extension (via pgvector/pgvector:pg17 
Docker image)
- init-chat-tables.sql — Creates chats and messages tables on startup 
with indexes on user_id, updated_at, chat_id, sequence, and timestamp
- create_tables-new.sql — Creates the 3 FAR data tables (asset_envelope,
facility, land_parcel) with foreign key relationships and indexes
- create_fca_table.sql — Creates a Facility Condition Assessment table (
component conditions rated C1-C5)
- Contains CSV data files: facility.csv (~1.2MB), land_parcel.csv (~
4.2MB), asset_envelope.csv (~1MB), fca.csv (~15MB)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
### 6. Data Ingestion (data_ingestion/)
- Python script (ingest.py) that loads CSV files, creates embeddings 
using Azure OpenAI, and stores them in pgvector via LangChain's PGVector
- Processes documents in batches of 100 with 1-second delays between 
batches
- Uses Azure Managed Identity for database authentication
- Has its own Dockerfile for containerized execution
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
### 7. Infrastructure & DevOps
Docker Compose — compose-base.yaml + compose-dev.yaml:
- 4 services with Docker profiles (can run individually or together)
- Shared volumes for hot-reloading in development
- Internal Docker networking (API connects to chatbot-backend via 
http://chatbot-backend:4000)
- Persistent PostgreSQL volume (pgvectordata)
Justfile — Command runner:- just run-dev — Starts all services
- just run-dev api chatbot-backend — Starts specific services
- just down (aliased as just kms) — Tears down all containers
- just build — Builds without cache
CI/CD Pipelines (pipelines/):
- api-container-deploy.yml — Builds and deploys API container
- chatbot-backend-container-deploy.yml — Builds and deploys chatbot 
backend container
- static-web-app-deploy.yml — Deploys React frontend to Azure Static Web
Apps
- data-ingestion-deploy.yml — Runs the data ingestion pipeline
- azure-pipelines.yml — Main pipeline orchestration
Production deployment targets Azure:
- Azure Container Apps for API and chatbot backend (with internal-only 
networking for the backend)
- Azure Static Web Apps for the React frontend
- Azure OpenAI for LLM and embedding models
- Azure Container Registry for Docker images
- Managed Identity for secure, passwordless access to PostgreSQL and 
OpenAI
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
### Communication Flow (End-to-End)
User types question in browser
  → React frontend (Azure Static Web Apps)
    → POST /api/chat/message to API (Azure Container App, public)
      → JWT validation against Microsoft Entra ID
      → Save user message to PostgreSQL
      → POST /query/ to Chatbot Backend (Azure Container App, internal only)
        → LangGraph ReAct agent processes the message
        → Agent decides to use SQL tool → executes query against PostgreSQL
        → Agent formulates natural language response
      → Save bot response to PostgreSQL
    → Return response to frontend
  → Typing animation renders the response word-by-word
IAM Legal Expert Infrastructure:
IAM Legal Expert Infrastructure
## IAM Legal Expert — Infrastructure Summary
This Terraform project deploys an AI-powered legal document analysis 
platform on Azure, hosted in the South Africa North region. It supports 
three environments: dev, stg, and prod.
### Networking
- A VNet with three subnets: one for Container Apps, one for PostgreSQL 
(with service delegation), and one for a jumpbox VM
- Private DNS zone for PostgreSQL, linked to the VNet so containers can 
resolve the private DB endpoint
### Compute — Container Apps
Four containerised workloads run inside a Container App Environment on 
the containers subnet:
- **Neo4j** (2 CPU / 4 GB) — Graph database service that downloads PDFs 
from blob storage using managed identity and builds a knowledge graph of
legal entities and relationships
- **Backend** (1 CPU / 2 GB) — Core application service that connects to
both PostgreSQL databases, Azure OpenAI, and the Neo4j service
- **API** (0.25 CPU / 0.5 GB) — Public-facing API gateway that proxies 
requests to the backend, with Entra ID (Azure AD) token validation
- **PgVector Job** — A manually-triggered container job that reads 
documents from an Azure File Share mount and generates vector embeddings
using Azure OpenAI, storing them in the primary PostgreSQL database
### Databases
- **Primary PostgreSQL** — Private (no public access), Entra-only auth, 
pgvector extension enabled, used for vector embeddings. The backend 
managed identity is the AD administrator.
- **Vector PostgreSQL** — Public access, both password and Entra AD 
auth, pgvector extension enabled. The backend managed identity is also 
the AD administrator. Connection credentials are passed to the backend 
container as environment variables.
### AI / ML
- **Azure OpenAI** (S0 tier) with two model deployments:
  - gpt-4o-mini — for chat/LLM inference
  - text-embedding-ada-002 — for generating vector embeddings
- The backend managed identity has Cognitive Services OpenAI User role 
on the OpenAI account
### Storage
- **Storage Account** (Standard GRS) with an Azure File Share for document storage (50 GB quota), mounted into the Container App 
Environment for the PgVector job
- The backend managed identity has Storage Blob Data Reader on the 
storage account so the Neo4j service can download PDFs via the Azure 
Blob SDK
### Identity & Auth
- **Backend Managed Identity** — A user-assigned identity shared across 
the backend container, Neo4j container, PgVector job, and jumpbox VM. It
has roles for OpenAI access, PostgreSQL AD admin, and blob storage 
read.
- **SWA Managed Identity** — A separate user-assigned identity for the 
Static Web App, with a federated identity credential linked to an Entra 
AD app registration
- **Entra AD App** — Referenced by name (app-iam-legal-expert-{env}), 
used for SWA authentication and API token validation
### Frontend
- **Azure Static Web App** (Standard tier) hosted in West Europe, with 
the SWA managed identity attached
### Container Registry
- **Azure Container Registry** (Basic SKU) with admin access enabled, 
used by all container apps and jobs
### Monitoring
- **Log Analytics Workspace** (PerGB2018, 30-day retention) feeding the 
Container App Environment
### Jumpbox
- **Linux VM** (Standard_B2s, Ubuntu 22.04) on the jumpbox subnet with a
public IP, SSH-only access restricted to a specific IP, and the backend
managed identity attached — used for debugging and accessing private 
resources within the VNet
IAM Legal Expert:
IAM Legal Expert
## IAM Legal Expert — Project Summary
The IAM Legal Expert is an AI-powered legal and best practices domain 
expert for Infrastructure Asset Management (IAM), built for the 
Department of Infrastructure (DOI). It allows users to ask natural 
language questions about South African asset management legislation, ISO
standards, and government frameworks, and receive structured, source-
cited answers.
The system is a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) application with a 
Knowledge Graph, deployed on Azure Container Apps within a private VNet.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
### Architecture Overview
┌─────────────┐     ┌──────────────┐     
┌──────────────────┐     ┌──────────────┐
│  Frontend    │────▶│  Frontend    │────▶│  Backend API     │────▶│  
Neo4j       │
│  Web (React) │     │  API (Node)  │     │  (Flask/Python)  │     │  Service     │
│  :3000       │     │  :8000       │     │  :5000           │     │  :8002       │
└─────────────┘     └──────┬───────┘     
└────────┬─────────┘     └──────┬───────┘
                           │                      │                      │
                           │                      ▼                      ▼
                           │              ┌──────────────┐       
┌──────────────┐
                           │              │  PostgreSQL   │       │  Neo4j DB    │
                           │              │  (PGVector)   │       │  (Graph DB)  │
                           └─────────────▶│              │       
└──────────────┘
                           (neo4j routes) └──────────────┘
                                    │
                                    ▼
                            ┌──────────────┐
                            │ Azure Blob   │
                            │ Storage      │
                            └──────────────┘
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━### 1. Frontend Web (/web) — React SPA
A React chatbot interface deployed as an Azure Static Web App.
Components:
- App.js — Main orchestrator: manages chat state, PDF selection, date 
filtering, and API calls
- ChatHeader — Application header/branding
- ChatContainer — Chat message display with typing animation effect
- SidePanel — Sidebar with PDF document selector, date filters, settings
(top-k, chat memory toggle, show/hide references), feedback form, and 
export options (PDF/Word)
- SampleQueries — Pre-built sample questions users can click
- DateFilter — Date range picker to filter documents by publication date
- KnowledgeGraph — Interactive graph visualization of entities and 
relationships, with GraphVisualization (custom rendering) and 
EmbeddedNeo4j components
Authentication: Configured for Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) via MSAL (
msalConfig.js), currently commented out for development.
Key features:
- Chat with typing animation
- PDF document selection/filtering
- Date range filtering
- Adjustable top-k retrieval parameter
- Chat memory toggle
- Source reference toggle
- Export chat to PDF or Word
- Knowledge graph visualization
- User feedback submission
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
### 2. Frontend API (/api) — Node.js Gateway
An Express.js API gateway that sits between the frontend and backend 
services. Deployed as an Azure Container App (ca-iam-legal-api-dev).
Key components:
- app.js — Express app with Helmet, CORS, Morgan logging, OpenAPI 
validation (via express-openapi-validator), and Swagger UI at /api-docs
- middleware/auth.js — Microsoft Entra ID JWT validation (verifies bearer tokens against Azure AD JWKS, checks tenant ID and access_as_user
scope)
- middleware/rateLimit.js — Rate limiting (global + stricter on 
/api/chat/query)
- middleware/errorHandler.js — Centralized error formatting
- gateway/backendClient.js — Axios client proxying to the Backend API (
BACKEND_API_URL)
- gateway/neo4jClient.js — Axios client proxying to the Neo4j Service (
NEO4J_SERVICE_URL), 10-minute timeout for bulk PDF processing
Routes (all auth-protected):
| Route | Proxies to | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| POST /api/chat/new | Backend /new_chat | Create chat session |
| POST /api/chat/query | Backend /query | Ask a question (RAG) |
| POST /api/chat/clear | Backend /clear_chat_history | Clear chat memory
|
| GET /api/pdfs | Backend /pdfs | List available PDFs |
| POST /api/feedback | Backend /feedback | Submit user feedback |
| GET /api/knowledge_graph/data | Backend /knowledge_graph | Get KG 
stats |
| POST /api/neo4j/ | Neo4j Service POST /documents | Add PDFs to 
knowledge graph |
| DELETE /api/neo4j/ | Neo4j Service DELETE /documents | Remove PDFs 
from knowledge graph |
| GET /api/health | Backend /health | Health check |
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
### 3. Backend API (/backend) — Flask/Python RAG Engine
The core intelligence layer. A Flask API deployed as an Azure Container 
App (ca-iam-legal-backend-dev).
api.py — Flask app exposing endpoints for chat, queries, PDF listing, 
feedback, knowledge graph data, and health checks.
rag_agent.py (KnowledgeGraphRAG) — The RAG engine, which is the heart of
the system:
- **LLM:** Azure OpenAI (GPT) via Managed Identity authentication (
AzureOpenAIChatModel — custom LangChain BaseChatModel that refreshes 
Azure AD tokens before each request)
- **Embeddings:** Azure OpenAI text-embedding-ada-002 via Managed Identity
- **Vector Store:** PostgreSQL with PGVector extension (via 
langchain_postgres.PGVector), storing document chunks with metadata (
filename, page, chapter, publication date). Database auth also uses 
Managed Identity tokens with automatic refresh.
- **Knowledge Graph:** Dual approach:
  - **Neo4j** (primary) — queried via neo4j_client.py for entity 
relationships, related entities, and graph visualization
  - **RDF** (fallback) — in-memory rdflib graph as fallback when Neo4j 
is unavailable
- **NLP:** spaCy (en_core_web_sm) for named entity recognition and 
relationship extraction
- **Chat Memory:** In-memory per-session chat history (keyed by chat_id)
Query flow (enhanced_query):
1. Validate inputs, check PDF selection and date filters
2. Build PGVector metadata filter (by source path and/or date range)
3. Similarity search against vector store (configurable top_k)
4. Extract entities from the question using spaCy NER
5. Query Neo4j for related entities (up to 2 hops) for context 
enhancement
6. For each extracted entity, query the knowledge graph for 
relationships
7. Vector search the relationship text for additional relevant chunks
8. Deduplicate all retrieved documents
9. Build a prompt with context, chat history, and KG relationships
10. Generate answer via Azure OpenAI with structured formatting 
instructions
11. Store exchange in chat memory
12. Return answer with sources, confidence level, and KG entities
neo4j_client.py (Neo4jServiceClient) — HTTP client that calls the Neo4j 
Service API. Used by the RAG agent for: health checks, entity search, 
relationship lookups, related entity traversal, graph visualization 
data, statistics, and Cypher query execution.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
### 4. Neo4j Service (/neo4j_service) — Flask/Python + Neo4j
A Flask API running alongside Neo4j Enterprise in the same container. 
Deployed as an Azure Container App (ca-iam-legal-neo4j-dev).
api.py — Flask app registering all route blueprints.Routes:
| Blueprint | Endpoints | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| health_routes | GET /health, GET /stats, GET /sample | Health check, 
graph statistics, sample data |
| document_routes | POST /documents, DELETE /documents | Add PDFs (from 
Azure Blob Storage URLs) to KG, delete documents from KG |
| entity_routes | POST /entities/search, 
GET /entities/<name>/relationships | Search entities, get entity 
relationships |
| query_routes | POST /query, POST /visualization, POST /related, 
POST /clear | Execute Cypher queries, get visualization data, get 
related entities, clear graph |
PDF Processing pipeline (document_routes):
1. Receive Azure Blob Storage URLs
2. Download PDFs using azure-storage-blob SDK (Managed Identity via 
AZURE_CLIENT_ID in Azure, connection string locally)
3. Extract text using PyPDF2, split into chunks
4. Run spaCy NER to extract entities
5. Extract relationships between entities (relationship_extractor.py)
6. Create Document, Entity nodes and MENTIONED_IN relationships in Neo4j
7. Clean up temporary PDF files
Services:
- neo4j_client.py — Neo4j driver management (read-only + admin 
connections)
- pdf_processor.py — PDF text extraction and chunk processing with NLP
- nlp_service.py — spaCy model loader
- relationship_extractor.py — Entity relationship extraction logic
Dockerfile: Based on neo4j:2025-enterprise, installs Python 3 + venv, 
pip dependencies, spaCy model. Runs both Neo4j and the Flask API via 
start.sh.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
### 5. PGVector Service (/pgvector_service)
A utility service for PostgreSQL with the pgvector extension. Contains:
- vectorize.py — Script for vectorization operations
- health.py — Health check endpoint
- Dockerfile — Container setup- Deployed via pgvector-container-deploy.yml
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
### 6. Database Bootstrap (/db_bootstrap)
A one-time initialization container:
- bootstrap.sh — Sets up the PostgreSQL database schema
- Dockerfile — Container for running the bootstrap
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
### 7. Document Corpus (/iam_legal_documents)
14 PDF documents covering South African asset management legislation and
standards:
- Government frameworks (GIAMA 2007, PFMA 1999, National Treasury 
instructions)
- ISO standards (ISO 55000, 55001, 55002)
- IDMS modules (Asset Management, Portfolio Management, O&M 
Management)
- Western Cape legislation (Land Admin Act, WCLAA Regulations)
- Infrastructure planning documents (WCIF/WCIS/WCIIP, C-AMP Guidelines)
- National Treasury Asset Management Framework (2021)
These are now stored in Azure Blob Storage (stdocsiamlegaldev / 
iam-legal-expert-documents container) and downloaded on-demand during 
knowledge graph processing.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
### 8. CI/CD Pipelines (/pipelines)
Azure DevOps YAML pipelines for each component:
| Pipeline | Deploys | Target |
|---|---|---|
| web-app-deploy.yml | React frontend | Azure Static Web App |
| api-container-deploy.yml | Node.js API gateway | Azure Container App |
| backend-container-deploy.yml | Python RAG backend | Azure Container 
App |
| neo4j-container-deploy.yml | Neo4j + Flask service | Azure Container App |
| pgvector-container-deploy.yml | PGVector service | Azure Container App
|
Each pipeline: builds Docker images → pushes to Azure Container Registry
(acriamlegaldev) → deploys to dev → manual approval gate → deploys to 
staging.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
### Authentication & Security
- **User → Frontend API:** Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) JWT bearer 
tokens, validated against JWKS with tenant and scope checks
- **Frontend API → Backend/Neo4j:** Internal VNet communication (no 
additional auth needed)
- **Backend → Azure OpenAI:** Managed Identity (
ManagedIdentityCredential with AZURE_CLIENT_ID)
- **Backend → PostgreSQL:** Managed Identity tokens (auto-refreshed)
- **Neo4j Service → Azure Blob Storage:** Managed Identity (user-
assigned) with "Storage Blob Data Reader" role
- **All services** run within the same Azure private VNet
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
### Infrastructure Summary
| Component | Technology | Azure Resource |
|---|---|---|
| Frontend | React | Azure Static Web App |
| API Gateway | Node.js/Express | Container App (ca-iam-legal-api-dev) |
| Backend | Python/Flask + LangChain | Container App (
ca-iam-legal-backend-dev) |
| Neo4j Service | Python/Flask + Neo4j Enterprise | Container App (
ca-iam-legal-neo4j-dev) |
| Vector DB | PostgreSQL + pgvector | Azure Database for PostgreSQL |
| Document Storage | Azure Blob Storage | stdocsiamlegaldev |
| Container Registry | ACR | acriamlegaldev |
| AI/LLM | Azure OpenAI (GPT + ada-002) | Azure OpenAI Service |
| Identity | User-assigned Managed Identity | Azure Managed Identity |
